title: National Identity Card — New Application
description: Obtain your first Smart NIC. All Sri Lankan citizens aged 16 and above are eligible.
documents_required: Original Birth Certificate
Two recent passport-size photographs (35mm × 45mm, white background)
Completed Form NIC 1 (from drp.gov.lk or any DS office)
Parent/Guardian's NIC (if applicant is under 18)
School certificate or principal's letter (if aged 16–17)
steps: Download Form NIC 1 from drp.gov.lk or collect it at your nearest Divisional Secretariat
Gather all required documents (originals + photocopies)
Visit your nearest Divisional Secretariat or DRP Head Office — go early and get a token
Submit documents and have your photograph and fingerprints captured on-site
Collect your Smart NIC in 3–4 weeks (standard) or 3 working days (priority)
tips: Bring photocopies of all documents in addition to originals — officers will ask for them
Arrive before 8:30 AM to get a token — queues fill up fast
The Smart NIC (chip card) is now standard issue — it's needed for almost every other service
Divisional Secretariats are generally faster than the DRP head office
common_mistakes: Bringing a photocopy of the birth certificate instead of the original
Photos with coloured or patterned backgrounds — must be plain white
Filling Form NIC 1 in lowercase — must be block capitals
Going without a token — the system is token-based, you cannot just walk up

title: NIC Replacement (Lost, Damaged, or Change of Details)
description: Replace a lost, damaged, or stolen NIC, or update your name, address, or other personal details.
documents_required: Completed Form NIC 2 (for lost/damaged replacement) — from DS office or drp.gov.lk
Completed Form NIC 3 (for change of particulars only)
Police report (mandatory for lost NIC — file this first)
Original damaged NIC (if damaged, not lost)
Original Birth Certificate
Two recent passport-size photographs (35mm × 45mm, white background)
Affidavit from a Justice of the Peace (JP) if reporting loss
steps: If lost: Immediately file a police report at your nearest police station
Get an affidavit from a Justice of the Peace (JP) if required
Download or collect Form NIC 2 (replacement) or NIC 3 (changes)
Gather documents including the police report and birth certificate
Visit your Divisional Secretariat or DRP office and submit all documents
Pay the replacement fee and collect new NIC after the processing period
tips: File the police report on the same day you discover the NIC is lost
Keep a photocopy of your NIC at home — it makes the replacement process much faster
Use Form NIC 2 for lost/damaged, and NIC 3 only for changing details on an existing NIC
common_mistakes: Applying for replacement without first filing a police report (for lost NICs)
Using Form NIC 1 (new application) instead of Form NIC 2 (replacement)

title: Passport — New Application
description: Apply for your first Sri Lankan passport. Valid for 10 years for adults, 5 years for children under 16.

offices.name: Regional Office — Kandy
offices.address: District Secretariat, DS Senanayake Veediya, Kandy

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original and photocopy
Birth Certificate — original and photocopy
Two recent passport-size photographs (35mm × 45mm, plain white background)
Completed application form (downloadable from immigration.gov.lk)
Bank of Ceylon payment receipt for the applicable fee
Marriage Certificate (for married women applying in married name)
Parent's NIC + Birth Certificate + Marriage Certificate (for children under 16)
steps: Book an appointment online at immigration.gov.lk — walk-ins are almost never accepted
Pay the applicable fee at any Bank of Ceylon branch using the appointment reference number
On appointment day, arrive 30 minutes early with all documents
Submit documents at the counter — biometrics (photo + fingerprints) are captured on-site
Collect your passport on the specified collection date (or request postal delivery)
tips: Book your appointment weeks in advance — slots fill up fast
Pay the Bank of Ceylon fee before your appointment, not on the day
The 64-page passport is recommended if you travel frequently — more visa pages
Photos must be taken at a professional studio — home prints are usually rejected
Many countries require 6 months validity remaining — renew early if travelling
common_mistakes: Trying to walk in without an appointment — you will be turned away
Forgetting to bring the Bank of Ceylon payment receipt
Photos with shadows, glasses, or coloured backgrounds — rejected on the spot
Bringing an expired NIC as your identification document

title: Passport — Renewal
description: Renew an expiring or expired Sri Lankan passport. You can renew up to 12 months before expiry.
documents_required: Current/old passport — original (will be returned cancelled)
National Identity Card (NIC) — original and photocopy
Two recent passport-size photographs (35mm × 45mm, white background)
Completed renewal application form
Bank of Ceylon payment receipt for renewal fee
steps: Book an appointment at immigration.gov.lk
Pay the renewal fee at any Bank of Ceylon branch
Attend the appointment with old passport and all documents
Biometrics are re-captured — bring your original NIC
Collect new passport on the specified date — your old passport is returned cancelled
tips: Start renewal 3–6 months before expiry — don't wait until the last month
Valid visas in your old passport remain valid — they're noted and transferred
Keep your cancelled old passport safe — it serves as proof of prior travel history
common_mistakes: Waiting until days before an international trip — Tatkal is expensive and stressful
Not realising expired passports can still be renewed (no time limit)

title: Driving License — Learner's (Provisional) License
description: The mandatory first step before getting a full driving license. You must practice for at least 6 months while displaying an 'L' plate.
documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original and photocopy (must be 18 years or older)
Two recent passport-size photographs
Medical Certificate from a DMT-approved medical officer (Form DMT 6)
Completed application form (Form B23) — available at DMT offices or dmt.gov.lk
Payment receipt for the learner's license fee
steps: Book an appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — select 'New Driving Licence'
Get your medical certificate from a DMT-approved doctor (list available at dmt.gov.lk)
Fill in Form B23 and prepare all documents
Attend your DMT appointment with all documents
Sit and pass the Theory Test — computer-based multiple choice questions on the Highway Code
Pay the fee and collect your Learner's License on the same day if you pass
Display an 'L' plate on your vehicle at all times while practicing
processing_time: Same day (issued immediately upon passing the theory test)
tips: Study the Highway Code of Sri Lanka carefully — available at bookshops and online
Your medical certificate must come from a DMT-approved doctor — non-approved certificates are rejected
A learner's license is valid for up to 3 years — plenty of time to practice
Book your appointment online to avoid wasting hours at the DMT office
common_mistakes: Not studying the Highway Code and failing the theory test on the first attempt
Getting a medical certificate from a doctor not on the DMT approved list
Bringing only a photocopy of the NIC — the original is required

title: Driving License — Full License
description: Obtain your full driving license after holding a learner's license for at least 6 months and passing a practical driving test.
documents_required: Valid Learner's License (must have held it for minimum 6 months)
National Identity Card (NIC) — original and photocopy
Two recent passport-size photographs
Medical Certificate from a DMT-approved medical officer (Form DMT 6)
Completed application form for full license — available at DMT office
steps: Ensure you have held your learner's license for at least 6 months
Book an appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — select 'New Driving Licence'
Get a fresh medical certificate from a DMT-approved doctor
Arrive at the DMT office with your vehicle and all documents
Pass the practical driving test — conducted by a DMT examiner on public roads
Pay the fee and collect your full driving license same day
processing_time: Same day (if practical test is passed)
tips: Practice in a variety of conditions including hills, busy roads, and reversing
The examiner will test: hill starts, reversing, parking, lane discipline, and road signs
Enrol in a registered driving school — instructors know exactly what the examiner looks for
Bring a well-maintained vehicle — bald tyres or broken lights will fail the vehicle check
common_mistakes: Appearing before the 6-month learner's license period is complete — you will be turned away
Practising only in quiet areas and then struggling on busy roads during the test
Vehicle not in roadworthy condition — check lights, horn, and tyres beforehand

title: Driving License — Renewal
description: Renew your expired or expiring driving license.
documents_required: Current/expired driving license — original
National Identity Card (NIC) — original and photocopy
Two recent passport-size photographs
Medical Certificate from a DMT-approved doctor (required for heavy vehicle licenses and drivers over 70)
Completed renewal application form
steps: Book an appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — select 'Driving Licence Renewal'
Gather all documents — medical certificate if applicable
Visit DMT on your appointment date and submit documents
Pay the renewal fee
Collect your renewed license — usually same day

fees.renewal: approximately LKR 1,000–2,000 (varies by class and renewal period)

tips: Renew before it expires to avoid late fees
Heavy vehicle (bus, truck) licenses always require a medical certificate for renewal
Driving with an expired license is an offence — renew promptly
common_mistakes: Waiting until the license has been expired for months — late penalties accumulate
Not bringing the original expired license

title: Birth Certificate — Certified Copy
description: Obtain a certified copy of your birth certificate. This is one of the most fundamental documents — required for NIC, passport, marriage, and more.
documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) of the applicant
Any previous copy of the birth certificate if available (for reference — not mandatory)
Written application with your full name, date of birth, place of birth, and parents' full names
steps: Visit the Divisional Secretariat where your birth was originally registered (fastest), or the RGD Head Office in Colombo (any district)
Submit a written application with your details and pay the fee at the counter
Collect the certified copy — usually issued same day at DS offices
processing_time: Same day (DS offices), 1–3 days (RGD Head Office)
tips: Get multiple copies in one visit — you will need them for NIC, passport, marriage, job applications
You can request a copy from any DS office island-wide, but it is faster at the one in your birth district
Birth certificates are in Sinhala/Tamil — if you need an English version for overseas use, request a translated copy
common_mistakes: Going to the wrong DS office — find the one that covers the area where you were born, not where you currently live
Not bringing your NIC — required to verify identity before issuing

title: Marriage Registration & Certificate
description: Register a civil marriage and obtain a marriage certificate through the Registrar General's Department.
documents_required: NICs of both parties — originals
Birth Certificates of both parties — originals
Two witnesses who are 18 or older, each with their own NIC
Divorce Certificate or Death Certificate of deceased spouse (if either party was previously married)
Notice of Intended Marriage form — filed 14 days before the ceremony
steps: File Notice of Intended Marriage at your local Divisional Secretariat at least 14 days before the intended date
On the registration date, both parties and both witnesses must appear in person before the Registrar
Sign the marriage register in the presence of the Registrar
Pay the registration fee and receive your Marriage Certificate
processing_time: Certificate issued on the same day as registration
tips: The 14-day notice period is a legal requirement — you cannot waive it
Religious marriages (Buddhist, Christian, Hindu, Muslim) have separate registration processes through registered officeholders
Get multiple certified copies on registration day — you will need them for name changes, bank updates, and passport renewal
common_mistakes: Not giving 14 days notice — registration cannot proceed without it
Witnesses forgetting to bring their own NICs on the day

title: Police Clearance Certificate
description: Official certificate confirming you have no criminal record in Sri Lanka. Required for overseas employment, visa applications, migration, and certain government and private jobs.
documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original and photocopy
Two recent passport-size photographs
Written application letter stating the purpose (e.g., 'for overseas employment in Australia')
Passport — original and photocopy (required for overseas applications)
Payment receipt for the fee
steps: Visit the Sri Lanka Police e-services portal at police.lk for online application, or go to your nearest police station
Submit your application letter, NIC, photographs, and payment
A background check is conducted — this takes 7–14 working days
Collect the certificate at the police station or receive it via email (online applications)

fees.urgent: higher fee — contact police station for current rate

tips: Apply at least 3 weeks before you need it — 2 weeks processing plus buffer
The certificate is typically valid for 6 months — check the validity requirement of the receiving country
Some countries (Australia, Canada, UK) require the certificate to be apostilled — contact the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for apostille
Apply online to save a trip to the police station
common_mistakes: Applying too late — 2 weeks processing time catches many people off guard
Not stating the specific purpose — the certificate must state why it was issued
Not knowing your police clearance needs an apostille for certain countries

title: Vehicle First Registration
description: Register a brand new or imported vehicle in Sri Lanka for the first time to receive a registration number and the vehicle registration book (log book).
documents_required: Invoice from the dealer or supplier
Customs Entry (CE) form — for imported vehicles only
National Identity Card (NIC) of the owner
Valid Insurance Certificate (at minimum third-party from an approved insurer)
Completed vehicle registration application form
Emission Test (VET) Certificate if applicable
steps: Book an appointment at dmtappointments.dmt.gov.lk — select 'Vehicle First Registration'
Ensure insurance is purchased before your visit — DMT will not register without it
For imported vehicles: complete customs clearance first and have the CE form ready
Bring the vehicle to the DMT office on your appointment date for inspection
The DMT assigns a registration number and issues number plates and the log book

fees.varies: Depends on vehicle type, engine capacity, and age — check dmt.gov.lk for the current fee schedule

tips: Reputable dealers usually handle first registration for brand new vehicles — confirm with them
For imports: customs clearance must be completely done before DMT registration
Insurance from an approved Sri Lankan insurer is non-negotiable
common_mistakes: Arriving without insurance — registration will not proceed
Not completing customs clearance before the DMT appointment for imports

title: Vehicle Revenue License (Annual Road Tax)
description: The annual road tax disc (Revenue License / OR) required for every motor vehicle on public roads. Must be renewed by January 31 every year.
department: Provincial / Local Authority (Municipal Council, Urban Council, or Pradeshiya Sabha)

offices.name: Your Local Municipal Council or Pradeshiya Sabha

documents_required: Previous year's Revenue License (OR) or the Vehicle Registration Certificate (log book)
Valid Emission Test (VET) certificate — from an approved testing centre
Valid Insurance Certificate (minimum third-party)
Payment for the annual revenue license fee
steps: Get the Emission Test (VET) done first at a certified VET station — not all garages are approved
Renew your vehicle insurance (must be current)
Visit your local Municipal Council, Pradeshiya Sabha, or Divisional Secretariat with all documents
Pay the revenue license fee at the counter
Receive the revenue license disc — display it on your windscreen immediately

fees.varies: LKR 2,000–15,000+ depending on vehicle type and engine capacity

tips: Renew before December 31 to avoid penalties — technically due by January 31 but queues spike then
Some banks and payment agents now accept revenue license payments — call your local authority
Book the emission test well in advance — VET centres get very busy in December/January
common_mistakes: Getting the emission test at an uncertified garage — it will not be accepted
Waiting until after January 31 — fines for overdue revenue licenses are significant
Forgetting to renew insurance before going for the revenue license

title: Grama Niladhari (GN) Certificates
description: Various certificates issued by your local Grama Niladhari (GN) officer. These are frequently required as supporting documents for NIC, passport, job applications, and bank accounts.

offices.name: Your local Grama Niladhari office
offices.address: Ask at your nearest Divisional Secretariat to find your GN division

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original
Written request letter stating the certificate type and purpose
Any supporting documents relevant to the specific certificate (e.g., birth certificate for residency)
available_certificates: Residency / Domicile Certificate (proof you live in that GN division)
Moral Character / Behaviour Certificate (confirms good conduct)
Land Ownership Certificate (for property within the GN area)
steps: Find out your GN officer's visiting days and hours — usually 2–3 days per week at the GN office
Visit the GN office with your NIC and a written request
State the type of certificate and the purpose clearly
The GN officer may visit your home to verify (for poverty or residency certificates)
Collect the signed certificate — usually within 1–7 days
tips: Find your GN officer's schedule first — they visit the office on specific days, not every day
The Residency Certificate is one of the most commonly requested — keep a few copies handy
GN certificates often have a validity period (e.g., 3 months) — get them closer to when you need them
common_mistakes: Going to the Divisional Secretariat office instead of the GN office — different levels of government
Not knowing which GN division you belong to — ask the DS office to look it up by your NIC

title: Voter Registration
description: Register to vote for the first time or update your voter registration when you move to a new address.
documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC)
Completed Voter Registration Form A (Form 04) — from GN officer, DS office, or elections.gov.lk
steps: Collect Form A (04) from your local GN officer, DS office, or download from elections.gov.lk
Fill in your full name, NIC number, current residential address, and electoral division
Submit to your GN officer or Divisional Secretariat
When the provisional electoral register is published, check online at elections.gov.lk that your details are correct
If anything is wrong, submit a formal objection within the published correction window
processing_time: Electoral register updated annually — typically registration drives in September–November
tips: Check elections.gov.lk first to see if you are already registered
You must be registered at your CURRENT address to vote at your closest polling station
Registration drives happen in the last quarter of the year — act before October ends
common_mistakes: Not updating registration after moving — you will still be registered at your old address and may have to travel to vote
Missing the annual registration window and being unable to vote at the next election
